label: User demand and task queue
evidence_scope: LLM products expose prompts, API calls, code-agent tasks, and enterprise workflows as visible demand surfaces.
allowed_visual_language: Incoming work becomes tokenized requests, queues, latency pressure, and service-level promises.
blocked_claims: exact workload volume; user adoption; productivity; business value; truthfulness
design_role: Starts the diagram with demand rather than chips so readers see the factory as a service system.

label: Training and post-training work
evidence_scope: Chapter 14 capacity split and hardware sources support training as a distinct synchronized capacity problem.
allowed_visual_language: Training consumes large accelerator, memory, interconnect, data, and scheduling resources to produce model weights and behavior.
blocked_claims: exact FLOPs; training cost; cluster size; utilization; model quality; energy per run
design_role: Separates build-time model creation from run-time serving.

label: Model weights and release gate
evidence_scope: Existing model/evaluation assets support the idea that trained artifacts move through evaluation and release gates before broad serving.
allowed_visual_language: Weights and system behavior pass through eval, safety, packaging, and deployment decisions before they become products.
blocked_claims: solved safety; benchmark crown; hidden chain-of-thought access; release causality
design_role: Gives the spread a hinge between training and inference.

label: Inference serving and scheduling
evidence_scope: NVIDIA/GTC and existing stack visuals support inference as a visible AI-factory workload when attributed.
allowed_visual_language: Serving turns prompts plus weights into streamed tokens through batching, caching, routing, and latency-aware scheduling.
blocked_claims: exact throughput; neutral performance ratios; exact cost per token; utilization; latency guarantee
design_role: Shows how the factory makes output after the model exists.

label: Accelerators, CPUs, HBM, and rack-scale systems
evidence_scope: Primary product/platform sources and Chapter 14 diagrams support the hardware/software substrate with caveats.
allowed_visual_language: GPUs, CPUs, memory, interconnect endpoints, and rack-scale systems provide the compute surface for training and inference.
blocked_claims: performance crown; availability; supply share; exact TCO; customer allocation; shipped roadmap proof
design_role: Anchors the machine in hardware without turning product claims into validation.

label: Scale-up, scale-out, storage, and data movement
evidence_scope: Existing networking/interconnect rows support data movement as a necessary system layer.
allowed_visual_language: Fast links, switches, storage, and orchestration move activations, parameters, retrieval payloads, logs, and service traffic.
blocked_claims: exact bandwidth; speedup; bottleneck ranking; deployment scale; universal architecture
design_role: Prevents the diagram from pretending chips work alone.

label: CUDA, libraries, serving stack, and control plane
evidence_scope: Chapter 14 CUDA stack and Chapter 8 inference cost stack support software as infrastructure, not mere code garnish.
allowed_visual_language: Frameworks, kernels, libraries, runtime, schedulers, observability, and policy controls shape usable capacity.
blocked_claims: market dominance; quantified lock-in; universal developer preference; exact margin; governance adequacy
design_role: Makes software a layer of industrial capacity.

label: Cooling, racks, physical plant, and site operations
evidence_scope: DSX and infrastructure sources support facility/cooling as constraint language under source-scoped caveats.
allowed_visual_language: Racks, heat rejection, floor space, backup systems, water or liquid-cooling choices, and operations mediate installed compute.
blocked_claims: site efficiency; water use; PUE generalization; customer deployment; facility performance; universal liquid cooling
design_role: Connects the AI factory metaphor to the building and thermal system.

label: Grid interconnection, electricity supply, and flexibility
evidence_scope: Power reports and scenario assets support demand, local concentration, and interconnection constraints.
allowed_visual_language: Electricity, substations, transformers, interconnection studies, procurement, and flexibility choices bound how fast capacity can become useful.
blocked_claims: delivered electrons; named-campus workload; emissions outcome; energy-per-token estimate; forecast as history
design_role: Shows the physical bottleneck under the software output.

label: Token output, answer stream, and economic meter
evidence_scope: Sources support token output as a visible software result and NVIDIA's token/revenue language only as source-actor framing.
allowed_visual_language: Tokens, code diffs, assistant answers, tool calls, and billing meters are the visible output of an industrial stack.
blocked_claims: revenue proof; cost-per-token; profit margin; productivity; model quality; independent AI-factory economics
design_role: Closes the spread with output while refusing unsupported economics.
